arts.ttl: Why Ollie Watkins hasn't been starting for Aston Villa as Unai Emery makes 'angry' admission
arts.sum: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery prefers that Ollie Watkins is "angry" about his recent lack of starts, rather than sad. Why Ollie Watkins hasn't been starting for Aston Villa as Unai Emery makes 'angry' admission

Fixtures Aston Villa U21 Vs. Liverpool U21 - Fri, Apr 18th 2025, 19:00 Aston Villa concluded their Premier League 2 campaign with a 2-2 draw against Liverpool on Friday night. Rio Ngumoha fired the visitors into a half-time lead at Bodymoor Heath when he converted with a low strike just before the break. Charlie Pavey and Mason Cotcher then turned the PL2 clash around within 15 minutes of the restart as Villa led on the hour mark. But Ngumoha registered his second of the encounter to draw the Reds level and it remained all square as both sides had chances to claim all three points, with the visitors duly securing their place in the play-offs. It ensured the Young Lions, who reached the round of 16 in both the UEFA Youth League and Vertu Trophy as well as the quarter-finals of the Birmingham Senior Cup, finished at the foot of the PL2 table. Cotcher fired a good opening off target from Pavey’s low right-wing cross after just four minutes. The visitors responded and Amara Nallo headed narrowly wide before Thierry Katsukunya blocked a goalbound effort from Trey Nyon and Oliwier Zych made a reaction save to thwart Ranel Young. But the Young Lions responded positively themselves and forced Kornel Misciur into a string of fine saves midway through the first half. Jamaldeen Jimoh-Aloba saw his strike parried by the Reds goalkeeper before Kane Taylor’s rebound was blocked, while Katsukunya’s 25-yard strike was tipped around a post moments later. Zych got down well to deny Young in the 35th minute but he was rooted to the spot shortly afterwards as the visitors opened the scoring, Ngumoha drilling low across goal and into the bottom corner. It ensured Liverpool held a slender one-goal advantage at the interval, which was wiped out within nine minutes of the restart. Captain Aidan Borland saw his strike parried by Misciur and Pavey was on hand to convert the rebound with a well-taken lofted finish amid a crowded penalty area. And Villa turned the clash around on the hour mark as Cotcher was first to react and convert after George Hemmings’ strike was saved by Misciur. Yet Liverpool levelled in the 75th minute as they pounced on a defensive error, substitute Keyrol Figueroa laying the ball off for Ngumoha to slot into the bottom corner. Both sides went in search of a late winner, Triston Rowe making a crucial block to deny Ngumoha his hat-trick in the 87th minute before Tyler Morton fired over when well placed. Villa sub Rodrigo Fortes then sent a 20-yard strike over the crossbar for the home side and Hemmings was thwarted by Misciur as the clash finished honours even, bringing the curtain down on the Young Lions’ campaign.

arts.ttl: Jacob Murphy reveals three key reasons behind his blistering Newcastle United form
arts.sum: Newcastle United winger Jacob Murphy has put his blistering Premier League down to the Magpies' midfield trio of Sandro Tonali, Bruno Guimaraes and Joelinton. The man who netted an absolute howitzer against Crystal Palace in the 5-0 demolition said: "We’ve just found great rhythm and balance among the squad, we’re going into each game really confident and it obviously helps when you’ve got a trophy in tow.

arts.ttl: How success could be costing Newcastle their BRIGHTEST talent
arts.sum: However, the emergence of a steady midfield has led to limited opportunities for squad players including teenager Lewis Miley, who Romano claimed has attracted interest from Premier League and Bundesliga sides after finding first team minutes hard to come by this season. Miley will undoubtedly get more minutes next season with more games to play but a loan to a Premier League or Bundesliga side could see the teenager return as an infinitely better player than the odd sub appearance would grant him, giving Newcastle and Howe a tough choice to make this summer.
